explanation: <Bring about> means to cause something to happen. Formal equivalent: <effectuate> or <cause>.
<Carry out> means to perform or complete a task. Formal equivalent: <execute> or <implement>.
<Draw up> means to prepare a document. Formal equivalent: <draft> or <formulate>.
<Fall through> means a plan fails. Formal equivalent: <fail to materialise>.
<Iron out> means to solve problems. Formal equivalent: <resolve> or <rectify>.
<Lay off> means to dismiss employees. Formal equivalent: <make redundant>.
<Mull over> means to think carefully. Formal equivalent: <contemplate> or <deliberate>.
<Phase out> means to stop gradually. Formal equivalent: <discontinue gradually>.
<Rule out> means to exclude a possibility. Formal equivalent: <exclude> or <eliminate>.
<Scale back> means to reduce in size or scope. Formal equivalent: <curtail>.
<Stem from> means to be caused by. Formal equivalent: <originate from> or <derive from>.
<Touch on> means to mention briefly. Formal equivalent: <allude to>.
<Set forth> means to present or explain clearly. Formal equivalent: <articulate> or <delineate>.
<Hinge on> means to depend entirely on. Formal equivalent: <be contingent upon>.
<Gloss over> means to treat something as less important. Formal equivalent: <minimise> or <understate>.
<Pan out> means to develop or turn out. Formal equivalent: <transpire> or <materialise>.
<Ramp up> means to increase significantly. Formal equivalent: <intensify> or <escalate>.
<Factor in> means to include as a relevant element. Formal equivalent: <incorporate>.
<Spell out> means to explain in detail. Formal equivalent: <elucidate> or <explicate>.
<Zero in on> means to direct attention to. Formal equivalent: <pinpoint> or <focus precisely upon>.
